title: Revival of Evangelion now available on Bandai Channel
display_title: Revival of Evangelion now available on Bandai Channel
wikitext:
[]: Revival of Evangelion now available on Bandai Channel             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Aug 07, 2015 8:45 pm Neat! I'm guessing it's not the BD version though... tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com'))

[]: Re: Revival of Evangelion now available on Bandai Channel             Quote Post by shinhito » Sat Aug 08, 2015 2:42 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Neat! I'm guessing it's not the BD version though... tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com'))

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sun Aug 09, 2015 10:05 am Bandai? The toy company? I mean, I can believe them having a streaming service, but it seems weird to me that they'd stream EoE. Isn't that a bit mature for the audience that buys their toys?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Sun Aug 09, 2015 10:46 am Bandai isn't just a toy company, they are a giant corporation with sprawling tentacles touching every sector of entertainment. Bandai owns Sunrise, which is one of the biggest animation studios and responsible for some of the biggest anime franchises. Bandai Visual is the media publishing arm that has one of the biggest catalogs of anime spanning more than half a century. Bandai Channel is a digital content delivery platform that is open to other publishers as well. Thus Khara/King Records is happy to use the audience it provides.

[]:             Quote Post by shinhito » Sun Aug 09, 2015 3:55 pm I wish I could find a fast enough Japanese proxy or something. I signed up and even paid for the rental to try to see the quality, but the proxy I used wasn't fast enough for streaming in the slightest. EDIT: Does anyone have any idea how I can access this site through a fast enough Japanese Proxy or something? I would love to check out the quality of this stream and I already spent money on it.

[]:             Quote Post by tempaccount » Fri Aug 14, 2015 5:14 pm Use http://www.vpngate.net/en/ , works pretty well.

[]:             Quote Post by shinhito » Fri Aug 14, 2015 6:20 pm tempaccount wrote: Use http://www.vpngate.net/en/ , works pretty well.
